title: 2026 Best Supporting Actor Oscar Odds: Sean Penn Wins
text: Sean Penn has won the Academy Award for Best Supporting Actor at the 98th Academy Awards for his role as Colonel Lockjaw in Paul Thomas Anderson's One Battle After Another. Penn entered the ceremony as the strong frontrunner on Kalshi's prediction markets, where traders had estimated his victory chances at 77%. His win concluded a competitive awards season race against fellow nominee Stellan Skarsgård, who appeared in the Swedish drama Sentimental Value, before Penn solidified his lead in the final weeks leading up to the ceremony. The film itself garnered 13 Oscar nominations and achieved a 94% rating on Rotten Tomatoes. Critics particularly highlighted Penn's antagonistic performance, with one reviewer noting that he threads the needle between truth and caricature in portraying a ridiculous human being who wants nothing more than the power. Pre-ceremony odds for Best Supporting Actor: Sean Penn 77%, Stellan Skarsgård 15%, Delroy Lindo (Sinners) 10%. The lead between Penn and Skarsgård had shifted three times since nominations were announced in January.
